---
title: "Your First Agent Session"
---

Get crosslink running and watch your agent handle a task with persistent memory — in under a minute.

![](../assets/img/quickstart.gif)

## 1. Install

```bash
cargo install crosslink
```

See [Installation](installation.qmd) for more options.

## 2. Initialize Your Project

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}
**You say:**

> "Set up crosslink in this project"
:::
::: {.column width="10%"}
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
**Your agent runs:**

```bash
crosslink init
```

This creates `.crosslink/` (config, rules, database), `.claude/hooks/` (behavioral hooks), and `.claude/settings.json` (hook configuration).
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Manual CLI equivalent"}
```bash
cd your-project
crosslink init
```

The interactive TUI walkthrough guides you through initial configuration.
:::

## 3. Start a Session

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}
**You say:**

> "What was I working on?"

Your agent picks up where the last session left off — handoff notes appear automatically.
:::
::: {.column width="10%"}
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
**Your agent runs:**

```bash
crosslink session start
```

If a previous session left handoff notes, they are displayed immediately.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Manual CLI equivalent"}
```bash
crosslink session start
# Read the handoff notes yourself
```
:::

## 4. Create an Issue

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}
**You say:**

> "Fix the login bug — it's high priority"
:::
::: {.column width="10%"}
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
**Your agent runs:**

```bash
crosslink quick "Fix login bug" -p high -l bug
```

One command: creates the issue, labels it, and marks it as the active work item.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Manual CLI equivalent"}
```bash
crosslink create "Fix login bug" -p high
crosslink label 1 bug
crosslink session work 1
```
:::

## 5. Work With Breadcrumbs

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}
**You say:**

> "Dig into the auth module and find the root cause"

Your agent explores the code, leaving breadcrumbs as it goes. These survive context compression — if the context window fills up, the trail of what was found and decided persists.
:::
::: {.column width="10%"}
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
**Your agent runs:**

```bash
crosslink session action "Found root cause in refresh_token()"
crosslink comment 1 "Token wasn't refreshed on 401 responses"
# ... implements the fix, runs tests ...
```
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Manual CLI equivalent"}
```bash
crosslink session work 1
crosslink session action "Found root cause in refresh_token()"
crosslink comment 1 "Token wasn't refreshed on 401 responses"
# ... fix it yourself ...
```
:::

## 6. End the Session

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}
**You say:**

> "Wrap up and leave notes for next time"
:::
::: {.column width="10%"}
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
**Your agent runs:**

```bash
crosslink session end --notes "Fixed auth bug #1. Dark mode (#2) is next."
```

The next session — whether it is you, another agent, or the same agent after a restart — will see these handoff notes automatically.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Manual CLI equivalent"}
```bash
crosslink session end --notes "Fixed auth bug #1. Dark mode (#2) is next."
```
:::

## What's Next?

- [Session Workflow](../guides/session-workflow.qmd) — deep dive into session management
- [Kickoff](../guides/kickoff.qmd) — launch autonomous background agents
- [Swarm Orchestration](../guides/swarm.qmd) — multi-agent phased builds
- [Knowledge Management](../guides/knowledge.qmd) — shared research across agents
- [Web Dashboard](../guides/web-dashboard.qmd) — browser-based project oversight
- [Terminal Dashboard](../guides/tui.qmd) — interactive TUI for browsing issues
- [Maintenance](../guides/maintenance.qmd) — pruning and health checks
- [Multi-Agent](../guides/multi-agent.qmd) — coordinate multiple AI agents
- [Hooks Guide](../guides/hooks.qmd) — behavioral guardrails for AI coding
- [CLI Reference](../reference/commands.qmd) — full command reference
